title: Gender and International Security: Research & Analysis | CSIS
gist: CSIS's gender-and-security hub frames women's changing wartime roles (Ukraine, Africa) and inclusive-institution reform (WTO) as strategic levers, and positions Women, Peace, and Security as a competitive advantage against China and Russia.

insights.claim: Russia's war is transforming Ukrainian women's societal role, with women taking on war-effort responsibilities that challenge traditional norms and could advance their position toward European standards and EU accession.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Wartime labor shifts can durably reshape post-conflict institutions and EU-accession trajectories worth tracking for reconstruction exposure.
insights.locator: Redefining Roles: How Russia's War is Transforming

insights.claim: WTO Director General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala argues a reformed WTO should integrate trade with global health, climate action, and gender equity to navigate geopolitical shocks.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Signals the direction of multilateral trade-institution reform as the WTO seeks relevance amid tariff fragmentation.
insights.locator: A Conversation with WTO Director General

insights.claim: CSIS frames Women, Peace, and Security as carrying hidden strategic advantages for the United States in countering China and Russia, not merely a values agenda.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Reframing gender policy as great-power competition tooling shapes how defense budgets justify these programs.
insights.locator: Countering China and Russia: The Hidden Advantages

insights.claim: Defense-tech startup Vannevar Labs is scaling a mission to equip U.S. public servants with cutting-edge defense technology, per CSIS's Smart Women, Smart Power coverage.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Names a private defense-tech vendor in the government-facing AI/defense procurement wave.
